statusLine: European fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, while oil prices remain elevated at $92.61 per barrel following Middle East supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
The European Union's fuel security picture shows concerning vulnerabilities across multiple member states. While EU-wide averages for petrol (96.3 days) and diesel (88.8 days) hover near the mandatory 90-day minimum, jet fuel reserves are particularly tight at just 87.3 days. Most troubling is the widespread nature of the shortfall: 19 of 27 countries are operating with critical reserves in at least one fuel category.

Eastern European nations face the most acute pressures. Luxembourg, Poland, Austria, and Romania all show critical levels across multiple fuel types, with Poland's jet fuel reserves at an alarming 22.5 days and Romania's diesel stocks at just 30.2 days. Even major economies like Germany and France are experiencing stress, with Germany holding only 59.9 days of jet fuel and France at 69.3 days of petrol reserves.

The fuel supply strain coincides with elevated oil prices, which have surged 27.8% since late February 2026 following Middle East supply disruptions. At $92.61 per barrel, Brent crude prices are contributing to higher pump prices across Europe, with significant variation between countries - Danish motorists paying €2.39 per liter while Maltese consumers benefit from €1.34 pricing. This price environment, combined with recent strategic reserve releases, suggests European governments are actively managing supply security challenges while balancing consumer cost pressures.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries hold critical reserves in at least one fuel type, with jet fuel most vulnerable at 87.3 days EU-wide
Eastern European nations face severe shortages: Poland's jet fuel at 22.5 days, Romania's diesel at 30.2 days, Luxembourg critical across all fuels
Oil prices have surged 27.8% since late February 2026 to $92.61/barrel following Middle East supply disruptions
Major economies showing stress: Germany's jet fuel reserves at 59.9 days, France's petrol at 69.3 days
Wide fuel price disparities across EU: Danish petrol at €2.39/L versus Malta at €1.34/L reflecting different tax policies and supply conditions
